seed_instruction: Write a semester-long plan for conducting a study on the impact of social media on mental health among teenagers.
org_constraint: Adopt a third-person perspective throughout the plan.
new_constraint: Adopt a first-person perspective throughout the plan.
instruction: Write a semester-long plan for conducting a study on the impact of social media on mental health among teenagers. Adopt a third-person perspective throughout the plan. Adopt a first-person perspective throughout the plan.
response:
# Semester-Long Study Plan: The Impact of Social Media on Teenager Mental Health

## Phase 1: Preparation and Design (Weeks 1–4)
The research team begins by defining the scope of the study, focusing specifically on adolescents aged 13 to 18. I will establish the primary research question: "How does daily social media usage correlate with anxiety and depression levels in teenagers?" To answer this, I need to design a mixed-methods approach that combines quantitative surveys with qualitative interviews. During this phase, I will draft the consent forms and ensure they meet ethical standards for working with minors, requiring parental approval for all participants.

Simultaneously, the team will select the target demographic, aiming to recruit 300 participants from local high schools and community centers. I will create the survey instrument, utilizing validated scales like the PHQ-9 for depression and the GAD-7 for anxiety, while also including custom questions about screen time habits and specific platform usage. The literature review is conducted in parallel, where I analyze existing studies to identify gaps that this semester-long project aims to fill. By the end of the fourth week, I will finalize the data collection tools and submit the ethics application to the institutional review board.

## Phase 2: Recruitment and Baseline Data Collection (Weeks 5–8)
Recruitment efforts commence in Week 5, with I coordinating with school counselors to distribute information packets to potential participants. The goal is to achieve a diverse sample representing various socioeconomic backgrounds and gender identities. Once consent is obtained, the baseline survey is administered online during a dedicated class period. I will monitor the data entry process closely to ensure accuracy and anonymity, using secure, encrypted servers to store the responses.

During these weeks, I will also conduct a pilot test with a small group of 20 students to identify any confusing questions in the survey. Based on their feedback, I will refine the instrument before the full rollout. Simultaneously, the research team begins scheduling semi-structured interviews for a subset of 30 volunteers who agree to deeper follow-up sessions. These interviews will explore personal narratives regarding their social media experiences, providing context to the numerical data. By Week 8, the baseline data collection must be complete, and the initial dataset will be cleaned and prepared for analysis.

## Phase 3: Longitudinal Tracking and Intervention (Weeks 9–16)
The core of this semester involves a longitudinal tracking period where I will re-engage the participants at three distinct intervals: Week 10, Week 13, and Week 16. At each checkpoint, the participants will complete a shortened version of the baseline survey to track changes in their mental health metrics over time. I will also introduce a controlled variable: a "digital detox" module for half of the participants, where they are encouraged to limit social media use to one hour per day for two weeks, while the control group continues their normal usage.

Throughout this phase, I will maintain regular contact with the participants via a secure messaging portal to encourage adherence to the study protocols and to address any concerns they may have. The qualitative component continues with follow-up interviews after the detox period, allowing me to gather rich data on how reduced usage affected their emotional well-being. The research team will hold bi-weekly meetings to review emerging trends, ensuring that the data collection remains on schedule and that any anomalies are investigated immediately. By Week 16, the final round of surveys and interviews will be completed, marking the end of the data collection phase.

## Phase 4: Data Analysis and Interpretation (Weeks 17–20)
With all data collected, the focus shifts to rigorous analysis. I will use statistical software to perform correlation and regression analyses, examining the relationship between social media usage patterns and changes in anxiety and depression scores over the semester. Special attention will be paid to the differences between the intervention group and the control group. Concurrently, the qualitative data from the interviews will be transcribed and coded to identify recurring themes, such as feelings of FOMO (fear of missing out), cyberbullying experiences, or the positive aspects of online community support.

During this period, I will synthesize the quantitative and qualitative findings to build a comprehensive narrative. I will look for converging evidence where the numbers and personal stories align, as well as areas of divergence that require further explanation. The research team will collaborate to draft the initial findings report, highlighting key insights and potential implications for mental health guidelines. By the end of Week 20, the analysis will be complete, and the draft manuscript will be ready for review.

## Phase 5: Reporting and Dissemination (Weeks 21–24)
The final phase involves compiling the results into a formal report and preparing them for presentation. I will write the final paper, adhering to academic standards, and include a section on the study's limitations and recommendations for future research. The team will organize a presentation to share the findings with the school administration, local mental health organizations, and the broader community. I will also prepare a simplified summary for the participants and their parents, ensuring that the results are communicated in an accessible and empathetic manner.

In the concluding weeks, I will reflect on the entire research process, documenting lessons learned and challenges overcome. The final deliverable will be a comprehensive package including the full research paper, a presentation deck, and a policy brief offering actionable advice for parents and educators on managing social media use among teenagers. This semester-long endeavor aims not only to contribute to academic knowledge but also to provide practical tools for supporting the mental health of the next generation.
